titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Collection of Motion Picture Stills PASC.0195
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by UCLA Library Special Collections staff.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-06-02 11:49:14 -0700.

did.abstract: Collection primarily consists of American motion picture stills, which includes black and white photographs, as well as some color prints, slides, and publicity stills from various studios.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

Portions of collection unprocessed. Material in the Accession LSC-9999-019 backlog material series/subseries is unavailable for access. Please see LSC's Access to Unprocessed Collections Policy for more information about levels of discovery and access of special collections materials and registering interest in unprocessed collections.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS DIGITAL MATERIALS: This collection contains processed digital materials. All requests to access digital materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of John and Corey Doherty, 2005.
Gift of J. C. Jessen, date unknown.

odd.head: Search the Collection
odd.p: The container list for this collection is not available via the OAC. The container list can be found at: Collection of Motion Picture Stills. For more information, please contact UCLA Library Special Collections.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Collection of Motion Picture Stills (Collection PASC 195). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by UCLA Library Special Collections staff.
Digital materials were received on 1 optical disc. All digital file naming, organization, and arrangement created by Warner Brothers was retained.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: History
bioghist.p: The history for this collection is not available at this time.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Collection primarily consists of American motion picture stills. Includes black and white photographs, as well as some color prints and slides. Also includes publicity stills from various studios. The bulk of the collection dates from the early 1920s to the present.

physdesc.extent: (8 unprocessed flat boxes, 11 unprocessed record cartons, 18 unprocessed doc boxes, 5 unprocessed half doc boxes)

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS DIGITAL MATERIALS: This series contains processed digital materials. All requests to access digital materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page. CONTAINS

UNPROCESSED AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Digital materials were received on 1 optical disc. All digital file naming, organization, and arrangement created by Warner Brothers was retained.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Addition to the Collection of Motion Picture Stills (PASC-0195). Materials consist of stills from motion pictures and television productions in a variety of formats, including printed photos, negatives, and transparencies.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Unprocessed series. With the exception of digital material, this series is unavailable for access. Please see LSC's Access to Unprocessed Collections Policy for more information about levels of discovery and access of special collections materials and registering interest in unprocessed collections.
